title: Backward-compat error message labels new format as old
description: When user supplies secretx://env/MY_VAR, parse() emits: 'URI uses the old `secretx:backend:path` format; use `secretx:backend:/path` instead'. Both halves are wrong: (1) the 'old' format shown IS the new format; (2) 'use .../path' prescribes a leading slash that is wrong for non-absolute-path backends (env, wolfhsm, pkcs11 etc.) — following the hint for secretx://env/MY_VAR produces secretx:env:/MY_VAR which looks for env var '/MY_VAR' (not found). Fix the message to say: 'URI uses the old `secretx://backend/path` format; use `secretx:backend:path` instead (see MIGRATION.md)'.
